packaged_context_block:
## Context Minor GC Working Set
Task state summary:
Earlier discussion established that OpenClaw had not yet shown clear user-visible usability gains or obvious context drop after topic switches; a dev log was written, and the conversation then shifted into a deeper acti…

Latest user ask:
- 为什么是这个建议

transcript.content: 那openclaw 长对话 context 最大的部分 是不是已经被接管了呢？
结论：**大概率是，OpenClaw 长对话里最大的那块动态 context，已经被接管了。** 原因很直接：
ok，当前的问题汇总下，写入到开发日志中； 完成后我们开始对openclaw进行测试；

当前问题汇总已经写入开发日志：[2026-04-19-clarify-host-level-context-minor-gc-acceptance-and-openclaw-feasibility.md](/Users/redcreen/Project/unified-memory-core/docs/devlog/2026-04-19-clarify-host-level-context-minor-gc-acceptance-and…
你刚才验证后，openclaw 是否达到了我们认为的用户端明显可用么/？ 整体的context 大小有明显的下降么？ （切不同话题）
还不能这么说。按我们刚才真正验证到的东西，答案是： - `OpenClaw 还不能判定为“用户端已经明显可用”` - `也没有证据证明整体 context 在切不同话题后有明显下降`

我们现在的任务是 深入的测试openclaw的 gc，是否能够达标；，尽量更长的对话（可以做临时数据写入，但是不要对我其他文档做写入操作）；做好探针，记录好相关数据；预期是：更长的对话，如果正常情况下，可能要做compact了，但是因为又了gc，实际不需要compact；同时在一个同一话题的长对话后切换话题，应该有明显的context回落；这里的context指的是实际发给llm的数据； 接下来你来策划这个测试，把相关配置都做好，然后就开始测试吧，最终简明扼要的给我结果和分析；

结论先说：**OpenClaw 现在已经能实打实地压住发给 LLM 的 prompt，但还没有达到“用户明显感觉切题后 context 明显回落、基本不用再考虑 compact”的标准。** 这轮我补了专门的长会话探针： - 脚本：[eval-openclaw-guarded-session-probe.js](/Users/redcreen/Project/unified-memory-core/scripts/eval-openc…
从新review下测试脚本， 没有问题的话，就要分析为什么没有明显回落，分析原因，找到解决方案；然后解决掉；

我先 review 了测试脚本和 guarded 主路径，结论是脚本口径基本没问题，问题主要在实现本身。 核心原因有两个： - [src/dialogue-working-set-runtime-shadow.js](/Users/redcreen/Project/unified-memory-core/src/dialogue-working-set-runtime-shadow.js) 里 `maxTurns=12`…
因为现在主矛盾已经变了，不是“有没有证据证明方向对”，而是“这个方向的代价能不能接受”。 具体说有三点：

decision.archive_summary: Earlier discussion established that OpenClaw had not yet shown clear user-visible usability gains or obvious context drop after topic switches; a dev log was written, and the conversation then shifted into a deeper active task: designing and running GC-focused long-conversation tests with probes and temporary data only.
decision.reasoning_summary: The latest user turn asks why the recommendation in the immediately preceding analysis was made, so this is the same active topic, not a switch. The unresolved task is still the OpenClaw GC investigation and fix path, so the raw turns around the testing task and recommendation rationale should remain in the next-turn prompt. Earlier status-check turns about whether OpenClaw was already 'usable' or whether context had dropped are now superseded by the deeper active test-and-fix thread and are good eviction candidates.

applied.archiveSummary: Earlier discussion established that OpenClaw had not yet shown clear user-visible usability gains or obvious context drop after topic switches; a dev log was written, and the conversation then shifted into a deeper active task: designing and running GC-focused long-conversation tests with probes and temporary data only.
applied.reasoningSummary: The latest user turn asks why the recommendation in the immediately preceding analysis was made, so this is the same active topic, not a switch. The unresolved task is still the OpenClaw GC investigation and fix path, so the raw turns around the testing task and recommendation rationale should remain in the next-turn prompt.
